title: bsc-finality-redesign
author: Li, Rujia
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This repository contains the implementation and artifacts for the paper:  Finality Attacks in Binance Consensus and a Provably Secure Redesign . 
 The paper analyzes the consensus protocol of BNB Smart Chain (BSC). Since mid-2023, BSC has integrated a  Fast Finality (FF)  mechanism, where validators vote to finalize block order. BSC claims that its FF mechanism can finalize blocks in O(1) time, simultaneously reducing latency and improving stability. The paper presents two attacks, showing BSC fails to finalize blocks in constant time and may even simply fail to achieve liveness. We further design and implement a provably secure redesign of the Fast Finality protocol, accompanied by formal analysis. The revised protocol removes the attack surface identified in this work and restores liveness without degrading performance. 
 This repository is intended to support both  functionality  and  reproducibility  evaluation, enabling reviewers and researchers to replicate all key experiments and validate the claims presented in the paper.
steps_raw:
This repository contains the implementation and artifacts for the paper:  Finality Attacks in Binance Consensus and a Provably Secure Redesign . 
 The paper analyzes the consensus protocol of BNB Smart Chain (BSC). Since mid-2023, BSC has integrated a  Fast Finality (FF)  mechanism, where validators vote to finalize block order. BSC claims that its FF mechanism can finalize blocks in O(1) time, simultaneously reducing latency and improving stability. The paper presents two attacks, showing BSC fails to finalize blocks in constant time and may even simply fail to achieve liveness. We further design and implement a provably secure redesign of the Fast Finality protocol, accompanied by formal analysis. The revised protocol removes the attack surface identified in this work and restores liveness without degrading performance. 
 This repository is intended to support both  functionality  and  reproducibility  evaluation, enabling reviewers and researchers to replicate all key experiments and validate the claims presented in the paper.
